SMALL BUSINESS ACCELERATOR — 10-WEEK GOVCONEDU FEDERAL CONTRACTING SYLLABUS (the full GovCon Giants / GovconEDU / AlchemyGov course curriculum)

COURSE DESCRIPTION: Designed to give small business owners the knowledge/skills to pursue and win federal government contracts — lectures, case studies, interactive exercises covering the federal contracting process, regulations, SBA rules, Buy American Act, and preparing competitive technical and price proposals (and the bid-protest process). Asynchronous online (Teachable), ~3-4 hrs/week, weekly Tuesday 10am ET office hours, certificate on 75%+ quiz average + good-faith document submissions.

WEEK ONE — Introduction to Federal Procurement: Does the government buy what you sell? (how/how-much/where/who, using free public websites); Prime vs Subcontractor roles; Are you ready to prime? (KPIs); Market-research-based strategies; entrepreneur perspectives (Angela O'Byrne/Perez APC; Steven Koprince/SmallGovCon top legal issues).

WEEK TWO — Small Business Fundamentals: Types of purchases (credit card, micro-purchase, Simplified Acquisition Procedures); moving from competitive to non-competitive (best value); federal procurement branding; avoid common capability-statement mistakes; live student cap-statement reviews.

WEEK THREE — SAM Registration and Certifications: SAM registration (free, required for prime awards and grants); NAICS codes (size determination); PSC codes; SBA profile / Dynamic Small Business Search (DSBS); Small Business certification overview (FAR Part 19 — SDB, HUBZone, WOSB, VOSB); maximizing each set-aside; award-winner perspectives.

WEEK FOUR — Contract Opportunities: SAM.gov searches (the Government Point of Entry for opps >$25,000); Sources Sought/RFIs (FAR Part 10 market research — who/why/how/where to respond); extracting and analyzing hundreds of SAM entries; Micro-Purchase and Simplified Acquisition Procedures (FAR Part 13).

WEEK FIVE — Teaming Partners: Limitations on Subcontracting (FAR 52.219-14 — prime vs sub work %); Joint Ventures vs teaming (13 CFR 125.8; FAR Subpart 9.6); finding a certified partner; spotting companies that need subs; subcontracting from primes (small business goals, SBLOs).

WEEK SIX — Market Research Essentials: Capture vs business development; finding government buyers (USAspending, FPDS.gov); what info to gather about agencies; Indefinite Delivery Vehicles (IDVs); SBA SUBNet subcontracting platform.

WEEK SEVEN — Business Development: Using the agency Forecast List; top 5 federal lead sources; finding and setting up an agency meeting (OSDBU/Small Business Specialists/SBLO); roles of contracting officers (and why they're NOT the gateway people assume — GS-1102 series); prepare for the call; choosing conferences/events; SBLO perspective (Amber Hanlon/Collins Aerospace).

WEEK EIGHT — Proposal Writing: Solicitation structure explained; how to format your proposal (Sections L & M); how to respond to a Sources Sought/RFI (FAR 15.201).

WEEK NINE — Proposal Pricing: How to estimate a government bid (justify pricing via GSA CALC, stay FAR-compliant); determining rates to charge (Davis-Bacon, equipment and labor rates); determining the subcontract % of work (FAR 52.219-14); pricing-as-art perspective (former Leidos capture manager).

WEEK TEN — Contract Compliance and Performance Management: Negotiations (FAR 15.4, profit); Termination for Convenience (T4C) clauses; Small Business Programs (FAR Part 19); notifications/protests/mistakes (FAR 15.5 — remedies when not awarded); contract cost / overhead rates (FAR Part 31 — allowable costs); Simplified Acquisition Procedures (FAR Part 13); dangerous subcontractor clauses (attorney perspective).

Plus an "Academy 3.0" supplemental video library for participants. This syllabus maps the complete GovconEDU federal-contracting learning track from procurement basics → SAM/certs → opportunities → teaming → market research → BD → proposals → pricing → compliance.
